subject: ap-calculus-bc
title: Table-Based Motion Analysis
stimulus:
Johanna jogs along a straight path. For $0 \leq t \leq 40$, Johanna's velocity is given by a differentiable function $v$. Selected values of $v(t)$, where $t$ is measured in minutes and $v(t)$ is measured in meters per minute, are given in the table below.

| $t$ (minutes) | $0$ | $12$ | $20$ | $24$ | $40$ |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| $v(t)$ (meters/min) | $0$ | $200$ | $240$ | $220$ | $150$ |

parts.prompt: Use the data in the table to estimate the value of $v'(16)$. Show the computations that lead to your answer. Indicate units of measure.

scoring_points.description: Correct difference quotient using the surrounding table values at $t = 12$ and $t = 20$: $v'(16) \approx \dfrac{v(20) - v(12)}{20 - 12} = \dfrac{240 - 200}{8} = 5$ meters per minute per minute

parts.prompt: Using correct units, explain the meaning of the definite integral $\int_0^{40} v(t)\,dt$ in the context of the problem. Approximate the value of $\int_0^{40} v(t)\,dt$ using a right Riemann sum with the four subintervals indicated in the table.

scoring_points.description: Correct contextual meaning: $\int_0^{40} v(t)\,dt$ is the total distance, in meters, that Johanna jogs during the 40-minute interval

required_elements: Total distance Johanna jogs from $t = 0$ to $t = 40$ minutes, in meters

parts.prompt: Bob is riding his bicycle along the same path. For $0 \leq t \leq 10$, Bob's velocity is modeled by $B(t) = t^3 - 6t^2 + 300$, where $t$ is measured in minutes and $B(t)$ is measured in meters per minute. Find Bob's acceleration at time $t = 5$. Indicate units of measure.

scoring_points.description: Correct evaluation: $B'(5) = 3(25) - 12(5) = 75 - 60 = 15$ meters per minute per minute

parts.prompt: Based on the model $B$ from part (c), find Bob's average velocity during the interval $0 \leq t \leq 10$. Indicate units of measure.
